mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS trend reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and raises the relative appeal of risky assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are not followed by higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than higher FEDFUNDS changes in at least 55% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses into a high-unemployment zone, expected household income growth and corporate revenues weaken, leading investors to demand lower equity prices and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 8.5% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases over walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals that both consumer spending and housing-linked demand are strengthening, which raises expected earnings breadth and produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both cross above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-HOUST trigger has a 60-day mean S&P 500 return that is not above the unconditional mean by at least 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend indicates improving capacity utilization and production volumes, which lifts expected operating leverage and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below a depressed construction threshold, housing investment and related durable-goods demand contract, reducing earnings expectations and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-700 triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero while UNRATE remains high, policy support offsets weak labor demand by compressing discount rates and liquidity premia, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS<=0.25% and UNRATE>8.0% regime has negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates fall and inflation-risk premia rise, causing short-horizon valuation pressure and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-scare observations have nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or do not underperform nontriggered observations by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 trend captures broad real-demand expansion that lifts aggregate sales and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to deliver above-unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward test windows.
